_about: ABP textual-variant footnotes (the dagger apparatus from the ABP 2nd ed.). Shape: book -> chapter -> verse -> [ {src, op, gloss, ref, conf} ]. src = printed Greek edition the variant comes from; op = adds/reads/omits; gloss = the English meaning of the variant word (the Greek form is omitted — it is unrecoverable as clean text from the source); ref = the word in OUR ABP text the note attaches to (so the reader knows which word it's about). conf = how the verse anchor was found (auto = exact text match; ai = meaning-matched). FILTERED: a note is only kept when its English actually differs from our ABP text — variants that read the same in English (a Greek-only or trivial difference) are dropped. Isaiah only for now (proof).
